_comment: Canonical output shape for a check-resolver rule. Three detection scripts emit JSON envelopes — check_pointer.py (2 rules), check_resolver.py (7 rules), check_evals.py (2 rules). Each script emits a single envelope (one rule) or JSON array of envelopes. Schema: {rule_id: string, overall_status: 'pass'|'warn'|'fail'|'inapplicable', findings: [<finding>]}. Per-finding: {status: 'warn'|'fail', location: {line: int, context: string} | null, reasoning: string, recommended_changes: string}. recommended_changes is REQUIRED — non-empty with concrete repair guidance sourced from resolver-best-practices.md.

findings.reasoning: AGENTS.md does not reference RESOLVER.md. The pointer in AGENTS.md is what directs Claude to consult the resolver before filing new content; without it, RESOLVER.md sits invisible.
findings.recommended_changes: Add a one-line pointer to AGENTS.md directing Claude to consult RESOLVER.md before filing new content. Example: `See RESOLVER.md for the routing table when filing new artifacts.` Place the pointer near the top of AGENTS.md so it's read in the first context load.
